Container.Name: SimpleLogin-AIO
Container.Overview:
SimpleLogin is an open-source email alias service. It allows users to create disposable email addresses (aliases) to protect their real identity from spam and data breaches.
What it replaces: SimpleLogin costs $36/year as a standalone SaaS, or is included in Proton Unlimited for $156/year. Hosting this on your Unraid server eliminates these recurring costs.
Required Companion Containers (Install in this order): 1. PostgreSQL Database (postgres-shared:5432) 2. Redis Cache (redis-shared:6380) 3. SimpleLogin-Postfix (MTA for inbound/outbound mail routing) 4. SimpleLogin-AIO (This container)
First-Run Steps: 1. Ensure your Postgres and Redis containers are running. 2. Start this container. The startup script will automatically run database migrations and generate DKIM keys. 3. Check the container logs to retrieve your auto-generated DKIM DNS TXT record. 4. Add the necessary A, MX, SPF, DMARC, and DKIM records to your domain's DNS settings.
Container.ExtraSearchTerms: email, alias, proxy, privacy, proton, forwarding
Container.Changes: [2026-03-21] - Initial massive template rewrite to expose 70+ environment variables natively.

Config.Name: WebUI Port
Config.Description: The port for the Web UI. Must be mapped to an available host port.

Config.Name: Email Handler Port
Config.Description: The internal port used by the SimpleLogin email handler to receive routed mail from Postfix.

Config.Name: App URL
Config.Description: The public-facing server URL for the SimpleLogin web interface. Expected format: http(s)://domain.com. If left blank, the app will fail to start.

Config.Name: Flask Secret
Config.Description: Cryptographic secret used by Flask to sign session cookies. Expected format: A long random string. If left blank, the app will crash.

Config.Name: Database URI
Config.Description: Connection string for the PostgreSQL database. Expected format: postgresql://user:pass@host:port/db. If left blank, the app will crash.

Config.Name: Words File Path
Config.Description: Path to the text file containing words used to generate random email aliases. If left blank, fallback defaults are used.

Config.Name: Temp Directory
Config.Description: Temporary directory used by the application for processing. If left blank, defaults to /tmp.

Config.Name: Email Domain
Config.Description: The primary domain used to create aliases. Expected format: domain.com. If left blank, aliases cannot be created.

Config.Name: Support Email
Config.Description: The address from which transactional system emails are sent. Expected format: email@domain.com. If left blank, system emails may fail to send.

Config.Name: Support Name
Config.Description: The display name used for transactional system emails. If left blank, the email address will be used as the name.

Config.Name: Admin Email
Config.Description: The email address designated to receive general system statistics and alerts. If left blank, admin alerts are disabled.

Config.Name: Postmaster
Config.Description: The official postmaster email address for the server. If left blank, standard bounce protocols may be rejected by strict receivers.

Config.Name: Email Servers With Priority
Config.Description: Defines the MX servers that custom domains must point to for validation. Expected format: [(10, 'mx.domain.com.')]. If left blank, custom domain validation may fail.

Config.Name: Postfix Server
Config.Description: The hostname or IP of the MTA (Postfix) server used to send outbound emails. If left blank, uses localhost.

Config.Name: Postfix Port
Config.Description: The port used to communicate with the outbound Postfix server. If left blank, defaults to 25.

Config.Name: DKIM Appdata Volume
Config.Description: Volume mapping for DKIM keys. This stores your generated cryptographic keys for signing emails. Must be backed up to prevent mail spoofing rejections.

Config.Name: DKIM Private Key Path
Config.Description: The file path inside the container to the DKIM private key used to compute DKIM-Signatures. If left blank, mail will be unsigned.

Config.Name: Other Alias Domains
Config.Description: List of additional domains that can be used to create aliases, appending to EMAIL_DOMAIN. Expected format: JSON array. If left blank, only primary domain is used.

Config.Name: Alias Domains
Config.Description: Strict list of domains that can be used to create aliases (overrides OTHER_ALIAS_DOMAINS). If left blank, uses fallback arrays.

Config.Name: Premium Alias Domains
Config.Description: List of domains reserved strictly for premium accounts. If left blank, all domains are free to use.

Config.Name: First Alias Domain
Config.Description: The specific alias domain used when automatically creating the first alias for a new user. If left blank, falls back to EMAIL_DOMAIN.

Config.Name: Disable Alias Suffix
Config.Description: Disables the forced '.{random_word}' suffix on new aliases. Highly recommended for self-hosting. Set to 1 to disable.

Config.Name: Max Emails Free Plan
Config.Description: The maximum number of aliases a free tier user can generate. If left blank, defaults to 5.

Config.Name: Alias Limit
Config.Description: Rate limiting for alias creation to prevent abuse. If left blank, no limit is applied.

Config.Name: Alias Automatic Disable
Config.Description: Toggles whether aliases are automatically disabled under certain conditions (like excessive bounces). Set to true or false.

Config.Name: Disable Onboarding
Config.Description: Disables the series of onboarding emails sent to new users. Set to true to disable.

Config.Name: Local File Upload
Config.Description: Instructs the app to upload files to local directory instead of an S3 bucket. Must be true for standard Unraid usage.

Config.Name: AWS Bucket
Config.Description: AWS S3 Bucket name for remote storage. If left blank, local storage is used.

Config.Name: AWS Access Key ID
Config.Description: AWS S3 Access Key ID. Leave blank if using local storage.

Config.Name: AWS Secret Access Key
Config.Description: AWS S3 Secret Access Key. Leave blank if using local storage.

Config.Name: AWS Region
Config.Description: AWS S3 Region. Leave blank if using local storage.

Config.Name: Disable Registration
Config.Description: Prevents new users from creating accounts. Essential for private self-hosted instances. Set to 1 to disable.

Config.Name: Enforce SPF
Config.Description: Allows SimpleLogin to enforce SPF checks using extra headers appended by Postfix. Recommended true.

Config.Name: GNUPG Home Volume
Config.Description: Directory path where the GPG Keyring is stored. Used for encrypting emails to users. Should be backed up.

Config.Name: GNUPGHOME Env
Config.Description: Environment variable pointing to the GPG Keyring folder.

Config.Name: PGP Sender Private Key Path
Config.Description: Path to the private key used to sign forwarded emails. If left blank, forwards are not signed.

Config.Name: Allowed Redirect Domains
Config.Description: List of domains permitted in the redirect parameter for absolute URL redirects, preventing open redirect vulnerabilities. If left blank, redirects are strict.

Config.Name: hCaptcha Secret
Config.Description: Secret key for hCaptcha integration on registration pages. Leave blank to disable.

Config.Name: hCaptcha Sitekey
Config.Description: Public site key for hCaptcha integration. Leave blank to disable.

Config.Name: GitHub Client ID
Config.Description: Credentials for GitHub OAuth login. If left blank, GitHub login is disabled.

Config.Name: GitHub Client Secret
Config.Description: Secret for GitHub OAuth login. If left blank, GitHub login is disabled.

Config.Name: Google Client ID
Config.Description: Credentials for Google OAuth login. If left blank, Google login is disabled.

Config.Name: Google Client Secret
Config.Description: Secret for Google OAuth login. If left blank, Google login is disabled.

Config.Name: OIDC Client ID
Config.Description: Credentials for Generic OpenID Connect login. If left blank, OIDC login is disabled.

Config.Name: OIDC Client Secret
Config.Description: Secret for Generic OpenID Connect login. If left blank, OIDC login is disabled.

Config.Name: Plausible Host
Config.Description: Host URL for Plausible Analytics. Leave blank to disable.

Config.Name: Plausible Domain
Config.Description: Domain name for Plausible Analytics tracking. Leave blank to disable.

Config.Name: Sentry DSN
Config.Description: Sentry endpoint URL for error tracking. Leave blank to disable.

Config.Name: Nameservers
Config.Description: Comma-separated list of DNS nameservers for the app to use directly. Prevents Pi-Hole loop issues. If left blank, uses host default.

Config.Name: Color Log
Config.Description: Applies colored logging formatting in Docker logs. Recommended true.

Config.Name: Not Send Email
Config.Description: Prevents email sending, instead printing the content to standard output. ONLY used for debugging. Set to false for production.

Config.Name: Enable SpamAssassin
Config.Description: Toggles inbound spam scanning using SpamAssassin. Requires a separate SpamAssassin container. Set to 1 to enable.

Config.Name: SpamAssassin Host
Config.Description: The IP address or hostname of the SpamAssassin server. If left blank, spam scanning will fail if enabled.

Config.Name: Bounce Prefix
Config.Description: The prefix added to the return-path for handling bounces. Must end with a plus. If left blank, VERP is disabled.

Config.Name: Bounce Suffix
Config.Description: The suffix added to the return-path for handling bounces. Must start with a plus. If left blank, VERP is disabled.
